title: Value type property used as input in a controller action should be nullable, required or annotated with the JsonRequiredAttribute to avoid under-posting.
description: Recommended Secure Coding Practices One of the main issues that under-posting can cause is data inconsistency. If the client sends less data than expected, the application might fill any value type properties with their default values, leading to inaccurate or inconsistent data in your database. Additionally, there might be unexpected behavior if there are certain data expected that are not provided and even security issues; for example, if a user omits a role or permission field from a POST request, and the server fills in a default value, it could inadvertently grant more access than intended.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects value type properties in controller input models without nullable, required, or JsonRequired

checks.message: Value type property should be nullable, required, or annotated with JsonRequired to avoid under-posting

logic.triggering_conditions: Value type property (int, bool, decimal, etc.) in public class without nullable marker (?), 'required' keyword, or [JsonRequired]/[Required] attribute
logic.exclusion_conditions: Properties with nullable type (?), required keyword, or JsonRequired/Required attribute
logic.reasoning: Value type properties default to 0/false when not sent by client. This can cause silent under-posting issues where missing data appears valid. Making properties nullable, required, or using JsonRequired forces explicit handling.

should_not_trigger: public required int Id { get; set; }
